stem: From a point Q on level ground, the angle of elevation to the top of a vertical tower is 45 degrees. From a point R, which is 20 meters farther from the base of the tower than Q along the same straight line, the angle of elevation to the top of the tower is 30 degrees. What is the height of the tower, in meters?
explanation: Let h be the tower height and d be the distance from Q to the tower base. Two right triangles share the tower height h. From Q: tan(45) = h/d, so d = h. From R: tan(30) = h/(d + 20), so d + 20 = h sqrt(3). Substituting d = h: h + 20 = h sqrt(3). Then 20 = h(sqrt(3) - 1). Solving: h = 20/(sqrt(3) - 1). Rationalizing: h = 20(sqrt(3) + 1)/((sqrt(3))^2 - 1^2) = 20(sqrt(3) + 1)/2 = 10(sqrt(3) + 1). Verification: d = h = 10(sqrt(3) + 1) approximately 27.32 meters. d + 20 = 47.32. tan(30) = 27.32/47.32 = 0.5774 = 1/sqrt(3). Confirmed.

distractorRationale.A: Treats the 20-meter distance between Q and R as the total distance from Q to the tower base, using only one right triangle: tan(45) = h/20, so h = 20
distractorRationale.B: Uses tan(60) instead of tan(45) at point Q (complementary angle error). With d = h/sqrt(3) and d + 20 = h sqrt(3): h/sqrt(3) + 20 = h sqrt(3), giving 20 = h sqrt(3) - h/sqrt(3) = 2h/sqrt(3), so h = 10sqrt(3)
distractorRationale.C: Correct: Sets up d = h from the 45-degree triangle and d + 20 = h sqrt(3) from the 30-degree triangle, solves h(sqrt(3) - 1) = 20, and rationalizes to get h = 10(sqrt(3) + 1)
distractorRationale.D: Correctly reaches h = 20/(sqrt(3) - 1) and multiplies numerator and denominator by (sqrt(3) + 1), obtaining 20(sqrt(3) + 1) in the numerator, but forgets to divide by the denominator (sqrt(3) - 1)(sqrt(3) + 1) = 2, reporting h = 20(sqrt(3) + 1)

imageDescription: A vertical tower stands on level ground with its base labeled T and top labeled P. Point Q is on the ground to the right of the tower, and point R is farther to the right. The distance from Q to R is labeled 20 m. A dashed line from Q to P makes a 45-degree angle with the ground. A dashed line from R to P makes a 30-degree angle with the ground. The tower height is labeled h.
